_doc: Casebook judgments. Two-pass authoring per AGENTS.md §Casebook authoring. Schema per entry: { id, chapter_unlock, draft, tags: { article[], principle[], context[] }, principle_moves[]: { id, effectiveness_modifiers, cost, name (Design), flavor_line (Design) }, judgment_name (Design), case_summary (Design) }. Tag values must exist in data/tag_taxonomy.json. draft:true entries are not loaded by casebook.gd until both Code and Design passes are complete.

judgments._doc: Beat 12 procedural-reset judgment. Triggers chapter1.court_won_procedural_reset via judge_district_ch1.json remedy_round_3_<stance> on_dismiss. Unlocked as a Casebook entry after court victory. Conditions: casebook_judge_state reaches 'round_3_remedy' AND at least one client_meeting_evidence item is present OR all three rounds are won without bonus evidence (modest-remedy discipline — the remedy is always procedural, never a full merits win). The actual remedy text lives in judge_district_ch1.json; do not duplicate it here.

conditions._doc: Judgment 'fires' (is added to Casebook) when all conditions are met. casebook_judge_state must have reached round_3_remedy. court_won_procedural_reset is set true by judge_district_ch1.json on_dismiss; this entry is unlocked at that moment. client_meeting_evidence is optional — its presence affects argument strength during the rounds but not whether the judgment is awarded.

bonus_evidence_variants._doc: Bonus evidence in chapter1.client_meeting_evidence changes the flavor of the Round 3 argument but not the judgment outcome. All three paths award the same judgment.
bonus_evidence_variants.wojcik_witness_statement: Round 3 sympathetic carrier — affidavit adds tenancy-validity texture to the rehearing-remedy argument.
bonus_evidence_variants.return_to_sender_slip: Round 3 blunt-procedural carrier — slip strengthens the wrongful-service finding underlying the remedy.
bonus_evidence_variants.lease_1962_inheritance_1987: Round 3 technical carrier — historical paperwork adds underlying tenancy weight; one-clause acknowledgment from the bench that tenancy is not before the court.

judgments.remedy_discipline: Modest procedural reset only. The client remains (procedurally, for now). The firm receives the 5,000 PLN Sikorska fee. The hearing is rescheduled; the merits remain open. Bonus evidence may strengthen argument strength but cannot upgrade the remedy itself beyond the procedural reset.

principle_moves.name: Motion to Set Aside
principle_moves.flavor_line: Service of process is the threshold. Below it, there is no hearing. There was no hearing.

principle_moves.name: Motion to Set Aside — Full Packet
principle_moves.flavor_line: Four elements on the file, in the order the statute contemplates. A non-current address. Prior knowledge by the petitioner. Actual notice within the window. No third-party cure. The motion is whole.

principle_moves.name: Actual Notice Window
principle_moves.flavor_line: The fourteen-day clock runs from actual notice, not from the defective delivery. We are inside that window. The statute says so.

principle_moves.name: Third-Clause Non-Cure
principle_moves.flavor_line: The current occupant of number seven is not her authorized agent. The landlord cannot bootstrap service through a stranger to the tenancy.

principle_moves.name: Rehearing as Remedy
principle_moves.flavor_line: The remedy is modest. We do not ask for a merits finding. We ask for the hearing she was entitled to before any of this began.

judgments.case_summary: A misdirected eviction notice — sent to an address renumbered eleven years before, by a landlord whose 2019 countersignature proved he knew — entitled the client to a set-aside and a fresh hearing she had never received.
judgments.memory_hook: The address was old. The landlord's signature was new. The gap between those two facts was the case.

judgments._doc: Article 8 misfit judgment. Picked up at Café Paragraf — a discarded brief from a previous client's case where Article 8 was the correct framework. Deliberately wrong for the Sikorska eviction: the issue is procedural (defective service), not substantive (disproportionate interference with home rights). Player who selects this judgment in court learns the article-fit lesson. Tags partially overlap with landlord_counsel_ch1 resists sets (proportionality, margin_of_appreciation) but not with weak_to sets; moves should land in not_very_effective or backfires buckets.

conditions._doc: Added to Casebook via overworld pickup at Café Paragraf. The interactable calls Casebook.add_judgment('home_and_family_ch8') and writes the pickup flag. No court-state gating.

judgments.remedy_discipline: Rehousing order or compensation for disproportionate interference with the right to home. Presupposes a substantive Article 8 violation — a proportionality failure in the eviction itself. Does not address procedural defects in service of process. Applying this remedy to a service-failure case is a category error.

principle_moves.name: Proportionality of Interference
principle_moves.flavor_line: An eviction is an interference with the right to home. An interference requires a proportionality assessment. No assessment was performed. The interference is unlawful on its face.

principle_moves.name: Family Continuity
principle_moves.flavor_line: Three children enrolled in district schools. A tenancy of fourteen years. Continuity is not a sentiment. It is a factor the court is obliged to weigh.

principle_moves.name: Margin of Appreciation — Narrow
principle_moves.flavor_line: The state's margin of appreciation does not extend to ignoring a family when signing the order. The margin is narrow where the home is at stake.

principle_moves.name: Private Life Disruption
principle_moves.flavor_line: The client's correspondence address, medical registration, and electoral roll entry are bound to this tenancy. Displacement is not hypothetical. It is administrative, medical, and civic.

principle_moves.name: Home as Convention-Protected Space
principle_moves.flavor_line: Article 8 does not protect housing policy. It protects this apartment, on this floor, behind this door. The client lives there. That is the only fact the Convention requires.

judgments.judgment_name: Dąbrowski and Family v. Poland — Strasbourg Chamber — Article 8 § 1
judgments.case_summary: A municipal housing authority evicted a family of five from a rent-controlled flat without assessing whether adequate alternative accommodation existed — the chamber held the interference disproportionate to the legitimate aim, because nobody asked whether the children had a school to walk to.
judgments.memory_hook: The family had nowhere to go. The municipality had nowhere to put them. Between those two facts was a proportionality gap the size of a classroom.

judgments._doc: Article 10 misfit judgment. Picked up at the Archive Room — a Murrow-flagged Strasbourg digest on press freedom. Deliberately and obviously wrong for the Sikorska eviction: freedom of expression has no bearing on a service-of-process dispute. Player who selects this has not read the case. Tags have zero overlap with landlord_counsel_ch1 weak_to or resists sets; every move should land in no_effect. The only way to backfire is if Step 2.3 wires an immune_to against one of these tags.

conditions._doc: Added to Casebook via overworld pickup at the Archive Room. The interactable calls Casebook.add_judgment('expression_and_press_ch10') and writes the pickup flag. No court-state gating.

judgments.remedy_discipline: Quashing of the penalty and declaratory relief affirming the right to publish. Presupposes a disproportionate restriction on expression — a fine, an injunction, a criminal sanction for speech. Has no application to housing, tenancy, or service-of-process proceedings. Selecting this remedy in an eviction case is not merely wrong. It is irrelevant.

principle_moves.name: Chilling Effect
principle_moves.flavor_line: The measure does not silence the speaker. It makes the next speaker hesitate. That anticipatory silence is the injury Article 10 exists to prevent.

principle_moves.name: Public Interest Defense
principle_moves.flavor_line: The information concerned the allocation of public funds. The public is not a bystander to its own budget. The interest is self-evident and the restriction disproportionate.

principle_moves.name: Prior Restraint
principle_moves.flavor_line: A penalty imposed after publication functions as a restraint on the next publication. The distinction between prior and subsequent restraint collapses when the speaker is the same person, in the same newsroom, facing the same fine.

principle_moves.name: Press Freedom — Watchdog Function
principle_moves.flavor_line: The press does not publish because it is permitted to. It publishes because the public requires it. The watchdog function is not a privilege granted by the state. It is a structural necessity.

principle_moves.name: Proportionality of Restriction
principle_moves.flavor_line: The fine exceeded the journalist's annual salary. The leaked document concerned a forty-million-złoty municipal contract. The proportion between punishment and public benefit is inverted.

judgments.judgment_name: Nowicki v. Poland — Grand Chamber — Article 10 § 1
judgments.case_summary: A local journalist fined for publishing leaked municipal budget figures — the Grand Chamber held the fine a disproportionate interference with press freedom, because the public interest in fiscal transparency outweighed the municipality's embarrassment.
judgments.memory_hook: The numbers were correct. The fine was for publishing them. The court found that accuracy is not a defense the municipality wanted to hear.
